data.body:
PASS 1 review — G6 (Approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection)

AFFIRMATIONS (criteria this plan satisfies):
- Fitness-for-purpose / solves-the-named-problem: the de-encoding half is exactly right. The probe really does lock the bug in as expected (scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh:1543-1547 — `fail_test "...unexpectedly synced inbound"` on the success path, `matrix_set ... NOT-SYNCED` on the gap path). Converting that to xfail/fail-when-the-gap-closes directly fixes the defect named in Why.
- Anti-change-detector: AC4 ("check round-trip outcomes, not whole-blob equality") is the correct, soundly-stated property and matches the convergence-assertion style already used in the codebase.
- Audit instruction is grounded and actionable: the only live `NOT-SYNCED`/"as expected" guards are at e2e_field_validation_probe.sh:1546-1547 (plus a doc mention at 1914), so the audit scope is tractable.

FINDINGS:

- [ ] Reconcile the plan with the EXISTING hermetic bidirectional round-trip harness before building a new one. tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_reconcile_roundtrip.py ALREADY chains the real pure differs end-to-end (outbound -> Jira-shape -> inbound) for comments (test_outbound_comment_not_reimported_inbound), links in BOTH directions (test_link_relationship_survives_roundtrip_without_reemit), and parent (test_reparent_outbound_then_inbound_converges); it runs in CI (`pytestmark = pytest.mark.integration`) with no live Jira, and asserts convergence OUTCOMES (no spurious mutation), not blob equality — i.e. it already meets AC1 and AC4. The plan does not mention it, so an implementer risks duplicating coverage or replacing a working harness. State explicitly whether the new test EXTENDS this file (e.g. by sourcing fixtures from story A's FakeAcliClient instead of synthetic `_make_ticket`) or supersedes it, and why.
  criteria: G6
  location: plan "What" bullet 1 / AC1 vs tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_reconcile_roundtrip.py
  evidence: existing harness module docstring "These tests chain the REAL pure differs end to end against mock fixtures (no live Jira)"; cases at lines 373 (comment), 425 (link both directions), 318 (parent); `pytestmark = pytest.mark.integration` at line 42.

- [ ] Resolve the mechanism gap: "story A's fixtures/fake through the production path" cannot, by itself, produce a BIDIRECTIONAL round-trip. story A's FakeAcliClient (tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/jira_contract/_fakes.py:31-63) exposes ONLY read/fetch surfaces — search_issues, get_comment_map, get_issuelinks_map, get_parent_map — and drives the inbound ENRICHMENT MERGE (its own docstring: "consumed ONLY by driving fetcher.fetch_snapshot / fetcher.compute_snapshot"). It has no outbound/apply surface, so it cannot generate "the Jira shape that results from a push," which is the load-bearing half of local->Jira->local. The round-trip MECHANISM lives in the differs (outbound_differ + inbound_differ) and applier.py:371; the existing harness models the push by computing the post-push Jira snapshot from `_map_local_to_jira_fields` + the applier's ADF/label/marker transforms. Specify how the new test obtains the outbound half (reuse `_outbound_jira_shape`? exercise applier.apply?) so the "bidirectional ... through the production path" claim is actually achievable, not just an inbound replay.
  criteria: G6
  location: plan "What" bullet 1 ("using story A's fixtures/fake through the production path")
  evidence: FakeAcliClient surface is read-only (_fakes.py:50-63); fake docstring scopes it to the fetch/enrichment merge (_fakes.py:14-17); the round-trip transform is reproduced in test_reconcile_roundtrip.py:_outbound_jira_shape (lines 122-142), not in the fake.

- [ ] Add a one-line POSITIVE rationale for WHY the chosen seam (story A fake + production fetch path) is the right vehicle for a round-trip assertion, given that an established round-trip seam (the chained pure differs) already exists. The plan asserts the approach but never says why this seam beats/extends the existing one — making the alternative-selection non-auditable. (G6 requires a positive rationale; it does not require a rejected-alternatives section.)
  criteria: G6
  location: plan "What" / "Why"
  evidence: ABSENCE — no rationale sentence tying the fake-through-fetch seam to the bidirectional round-trip goal; the existing differ-chaining seam (test_reconcile_roundtrip.py) is the obvious comparator and is unaddressed.
